title: MCP Apps — Embedded Interactive Tool UI
summary: Official MCP extension documentation for the Apps layer: ui:// resources, sandboxed iframe rendering, CSP and permissions policy, JSON-RPC host communication, tool-call approval, context updates, and guidance on when an embedded app surface is preferable to a normal link. Review frequently as the extension evolves.
lessons: Apply a hostile-boundary threat model for every MCP App surface: least-privilege capability grant, explicit CSP allowlist, origin isolation between host and embedded app, and tool-call approval UX before any external action.
Accessible iframe implementation requires: descriptive title attribute, focus management on open/close, keyboard-reachable close/fallback, and responsive host sizing.
Provide fallback content for every embedded surface so MCP Apps are additive, not required, for core task completion.
Define a teardown and cleanup contract for every App lifecycle event.
caveats: MCP spec is actively evolving; Apps extension is high drift risk — use 30-day review cadence and pin to specific spec version in conformance fixtures.
Vendor-specific host CSP policies may be more restrictive than the spec minimum; validate against each target host separately.
Fallback content and teardown behavior must be explicitly tested; do not assume host environments provide recovery automatically.
licenseNote: Official MCP documentation; cite and synthesize.
antiImitationNote: Adopt the hostile-boundary threat model and explicit CSP/permission patterns; do not assume embedded UI is safe without explicit origin isolation and tool-call approval.
